title: The Summarization Task
concept: Text summarization condenses long documents while preserving key facts, evaluated using overlap metrics.

exercises.prompt: What does it mean to summarize mean when we talk about 'summarizing' a document' tasks in AI. What does it mean to make text...

options.text: Making a text much longer by adding details
Condensing a text while keeping the key facts

exercises.explain: Summarization is all about making things shorter (condensing) while keeping the core message intact.

cards.title: Too Long; Didn't Read?
cards.body: Have you ever had to read a massive article for school and wished you could just get the main points in ten seconds? That is exactly what AI text summarization does!

cards.title: What is Summarization?
cards.body: Text summarization is the process of taking a long piece of text and condensing it into a shorter version. The goal is to keep the most important facts and main ideas while throwing away the extra, unnecessary details.

cards.title: The Movie Trailer
cards.body: Think of text summarization like making a movie trailer. Instead of showing the entire two-hour film, the trailer highlights only the most exciting plot points and main characters so you get the whole story in just two minutes!

cards.title: Summarizing a Space Report
cards.body: Imagine you have a 10-page report about a new space mission. A summarization AI can read the entire document and instantly output three bullet points highlighting the mission's launch date, destination, and scientific goals.

cards.title: Are Summaries Just Copy-Paste?
cards.body: People often think summarization tools just copy and paste random sentences from the original text. While some do (extractive), advanced AIs can rewrite the ideas in their own words (abstractive) to make it sound natural!

cards.title: Long vs. Short
cards.body: Let's compare the original long text with its summarized version. Notice how the core meaning stays the same but the fluff is gone!

left.text: Jupiter is the fifth planet from the Sun and the largest in the Solar System. It is a gas giant with a mass more than two and a half times that of all the other planets in the Solar System combined.

right.text: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system and the fifth from the Sun.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of the text summarization task?

options.text: To make the text longer by adding creative details.
To shorten the document while keeping the most important facts.
To change the facts of the story to make it more exciting.
To translate the text into a different language.

exercises.explain: The main goal of summarization is to condense the document (make it shorter) while preserving the key facts. Adding opinions, making it longer, or changing facts is not correct.

exercises.prompt: True or False: We evaluate AI summaries by comparing them to human-written summaries.
exercises.explain: Evaluation metrics typically compare the AI's output to one or more reference summaries written by human experts.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete how we evaluate summary quality.
exercises.sentence: To test if a summary is accurate, we calculate the word [blank] between the AI's summary and a human's summary.
exercises.explain: We measure how good a summary is by checking how many key words overlap with a summary written by a human expert.

exercises.prompt: An AI is asked to summarize a book. It outputs: "This book is amazing! I give it 5 stars because the ending was very surprising." Is this a good summarization?

options.text: This is a great summary because it condenses the story.
This is a great summary because it explains the main characters.
This is a review, not a summary, because it focuses on personal opinion and ratings.

exercises.explain: The AI output is not a summary; it is an opinionated review that rates the book, rather than condensing the book's actual content.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following scenarios is the best example of a text summarization task?

options.text: Condensing a multi-page news article into a one-paragraph summary.
Translating a German document into English.
Correcting grammatical errors in a short essay.
Generating a fictional story from a one-sentence prompt.

exercises.explain: Summarization takes a long text and condenses it into a shorter version. It does not generate new background knowledge, translate languages, or correct spelling errors.
